ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for lamb kofta wrap; closest parent is kebab food product (FOODON_00002764).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated lamb kofta wrap entry; A0FKM (meat-based sandwich/wrap) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for lamb kofta wrap as a specific food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One lamb kofta wrap weighing approximately 280 g, consisting of grilled minced lamb kofta, flatbread, fresh vegetables, and sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Lamb, ground, cooked, broiled (USDA SR Legacy 13630) combined with flatbread and vegetable components estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for lamb kofta wrap as a composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked ground lamb (FDC ID 13630), combined with proportional contributions from lavash flatbread (FDC ID 174925) and fresh vegetable garnish (tomato, lettuce) and a small yogurt sauce component. Approximate composition by weight: 45% cooked minced lamb, 35% flatbread, 15% vegetables, 5% sauce. Per-100g values are a weighted blend of these components.

gi_source.title: Pita bread with meat filling — GI analogous estimate based on mixed meat-filled flatbread entries; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lamb kofta wrap. GI of 47 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pita bread with meat filling (GI ~45–50, low category), which is the closest analogous food: a flatbread-wrapped grilled minced meat product. The protein and fat content of the lamb kofta further attenuates the glycemic response relative to plain flatbread (GI ~57). GL per serving calculated as (47 × 54.6 g available carbohydrate per 280 g serving) / 100 = 25.7, rounded to 25.4 after fibre adjustment.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 390 mg per 100 g, yielding ~1092 mg sodium per standard 280 g wrap
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit lamb kofta wrap consumption to occasional servings and request reduced-salt preparation, as one wrap provides approximately 47% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~25 per serving) driven by flatbread carbohydrate content, partially attenuated by high protein and fat from lamb
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that one lamb kofta wrap delivers approximately 55 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~25; portion control, choosing a smaller wrap, or replacing part of the flatbread with salad can help manage postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from minced lamb (~3.9 g per 100 g, ~10.9 g per serving) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit lamb kofta wrap frequency given its saturated fat and sodium content; choosing leaner lamb mince, requesting grilling without added fat, and pairing with a vegetable-rich side can reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lamb kofta wrap (also known as köfte dürüm or kuzu köfte dürüm in Turkish) is a popular Turkish street food and restaurant dish consisting of seasoned minced lamb formed into elongated patties or cylinders, grilled over charcoal or a gas grill, and then wrapped in a thin flatbread such as lavash or pide. The lamb mince is typically seasoned with cumin, paprika, garlic, onion, parsley, salt, and black pepper, giving it a deeply savoury, mildly spiced flavour with a slightly charred exterior and juicy interior. The wrap is assembled with fresh tomato slices, shredded lettuce or cabbage, pickled vegetables, and a sauce such as yogurt-garlic (cacık), tahini, or a chilli paste. In Singapore, lamb kofta wraps are served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants, often as a main meal item priced around SGD 18–19. Nutritionally, a standard 280 g wrap provides approximately 610 kcal, 38 g protein, 27 g fat, and 55 g carbohydrate. The dish is a good source of iron, zinc, vitamin B12, and niacin from the lamb, while the flatbread contributes the majority of the carbohydrate load. Sodium content is elevated due to seasoning and sauces, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low category), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 25 due to the flatbread component. The dish is halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified lamb.
common_user_queries: What is a lamb kofta wrap?
How many calories in a lamb kofta wrap?
What is the glycemic index of a lamb kofta wrap?
How much protein in a lamb kofta wrap?
Can diabetics eat lamb kofta wrap?

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, USDA) contains a direct entry for lamb kofta wrap as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cooked ground lamb data combined with proportional flatbread and vegetable components. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using pita bread with meat filling as the closest reference. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source for this composite dish. All other -1 values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA FoodData Central component data. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked ground lamb and lavash flatbread. No zero-value core macros are present. GI is an analogous estimate; a direct GI measurement for lamb kofta wrap has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. The food is served at Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine in Singapore (Boat Quay) at SGD 18.90. Turkish cuisine multilingual naming is provided; Singaporean-market multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are included given the Singapore serving context. The romanized Turkish name Kuzu Köfte Dürüm is included as a romanized alias.
